text: Had a great debugging session today, finally found the race condition in the payment service.
title: Debugging Race Condition

text: Morning run through the park was refreshing.
title: Morning Run

text: Need to clamp this value.
title: Clamp Test Low Importance

text: Need to clamp this value too.
title: Clamp Test High Importance

text: Spent three hours tracking down a memory leak in the session manager. Turned out the WeakReference cache wasn't being cleared when sessions expired. Had to add a cleanup hook to the session lifecycle listener.
title: Session Manager Memory Leak

text: Code review with Marcus on the new caching layer. He pointed out that our eviction policy doesn't account for hot keys under high contention. Need to look into adaptive replacement cache algorithms.
title: Caching Layer Code Review

text: The Kubernetes pod autoscaler kept thrashing between 3 and 12 replicas during the load test. Traced it to a misconfigured HPA target utilization of 50% combined with our bursty traffic pattern. Changed to 75% with a stabilization window of 300 seconds.
title: K8s Autoscaler Thrashing

text: Architecture discussion about migrating from monolithic event bus to partitioned Kafka streams. The team agreed on a strangler fig approach, starting with the notification subsystem. Timeline is Q3 this year.
title: Kafka Migration Architecture

text: Deployed the new rate limiter to production. Used a sliding window counter with Redis sorted sets. Initial monitoring shows 99.9th percentile latency at 2.3ms which is well within our SLA.
title: Rate Limiter Deployment

text: Found a subtle bug in the distributed lock implementation. The TTL renewal goroutine was racing with the lock release, causing a brief window where two processes could hold the same lock. Fixed with a generation counter.
title: Distributed Lock Race Condition

text: Weekday lunch prep: usually batch cook on Sundays for the week. This week it's chicken teriyaki bowls with brown rice and roasted broccoli. Takes about an hour but saves so much time and money versus ordering delivery.
title: Weekly Lunch Prep

text: Byte knocked over my coffee mug onto my keyboard this morning. Had to spend 20 minutes cleaning between the keys with compressed air. She just sat there grooming herself like nothing happened.
title: Byte vs Coffee Mug

text: Sam surprised me with homemade ramen for dinner. They spent the whole afternoon making the broth from scratch with pork bones. It was incredible — rich and silky with just the right amount of garlic.
title: Sam's Homemade Ramen

text: Sprint retrospective was tense. The team felt the deadline pressure from leadership was unrealistic for the data pipeline rewrite. I pushed back on behalf of the team and got us an extra two weeks.
title: Sprint Retro Deadline Pushback

text: Board game night with Leo and Mika. We played three rounds of Wingspan. Leo won twice with his wetland strategy but I managed to pull off a late-game food engine victory in the last round.
title: Board Game Night Wingspan

text: Went to the new farmers market in Capitol Hill with Sam. Found amazing heirloom tomatoes and a cheese vendor that sells this incredible aged gouda with crystals. Bought way too much but no regrets.
title: Capitol Hill Farmers Market

text: Sam and I had a long talk about whether to get a second cat. I'm worried about Byte's territorial behavior but Sam thinks she'd adjust. We decided to volunteer at the shelter first to see how she reacts to other cats at home.
title: Second Cat Discussion

text: Cooked a Thai basil stir-fry for the first time using the recipe from that YouTube channel Sam recommended. Used bird's eye chilies and it turned out perfectly spicy. Sam couldn't handle the heat though.
title: Thai Basil Stir-Fry Attempt

text: Video called Mom and Dad. Dad's retirement is going well, he's taken up watercolor painting. Mom is still adjusting to having him around the house all day. They want to visit Seattle in August.
title: Video Call with Parents

text: Helped Leo move his new bookshelf up three flights of stairs. That thing weighed a ton. He repaid us with pizza and beer. His new apartment has an amazing view of the Sound.
title: Helping Leo Move Furniture

text: Took Byte to the vet for her annual checkup. She was not happy in the carrier. Vet said she's healthy but slightly overweight — need to cut back on the treats. Easier said than done with those eyes.
title: Byte's Vet Visit

text: Finally finished reading Project Hail Mary. The ending with Rocky was so satisfying. Stayed up until 2am to finish it. Sam was annoyed I had the light on but agreed it was worth it when I summarized the plot.
title: Finished Project Hail Mary

text: Rock climbing session at Seattle Bouldering Project. Sent a V5 I've been projecting for two weeks — the crux was a dynamic move to a small crimp at the top. My fingers are completely shredded.
title: Sent V5 Boulder Problem

text: Did a 10K run along the Burke-Gilman trail. Pace was 5:15/km which is a personal best for that distance. Felt strong through the last two kilometers. Weather was perfect — overcast and 55 degrees.
title: 10K Personal Best

text: Annual physical went well. Blood pressure is 118/76, cholesterol is slightly elevated at 205 but doctor said it's not concerning given my activity level. Need to watch the cheese intake though.
title: Annual Physical Results

text: Tweaked my shoulder on an overhang route at the climbing gym. Nothing serious but need to take a few days off and do the rotator cuff exercises the physio gave me last year.
title: Shoulder Tweak Climbing

text: I remember building a treehouse with Dad in our backyard in Portland when I was about 8 years old. We spent three weekends on it. I handed him nails and he let me hammer in the last board. It felt like we built a castle.
title: Building Treehouse with Dad

text: First day of computer science class at UW. Professor Harmon wrote 'Hello World' on the whiteboard and I thought programming looked boring. By the end of the week I was staying up until 3am writing Python scripts to automate my homework.
title: First CS Class at UW

text: Moving to Seattle after graduation in 2014. Sam helped me pack the U-Haul in the rain. We drove up from Portland blasting old punk albums. The apartment was tiny but it felt like freedom.
title: Moving to Seattle Post-Graduation

text: Getting my first real engineering job at Cascade Labs. The interview was terrifying — they asked me to implement a distributed consensus algorithm on a whiteboard. Somehow I got an offer the next day.
title: First Job at Cascade Labs

text: My grandfather taught me to play chess when I was 6. We used to play every Sunday at his house in Beaverton. He always let me win until I was 10, then he started playing for real. I didn't beat him legitimately until I was 14.
title: Learning Chess with Grandpa

text: The summer I spent at coding camp when I was 15. Built my first website — a fan page for Firefly. It was hideous with animated GIFs and auto-playing music but I was so proud. That was when I knew I wanted to do this for a career.
title: First Website at Coding Camp

text: Left Cascade Labs after three years to join the startup Synth.io. Scary decision but the distributed systems work they were doing was exactly what I wanted. Higher risk but way more autonomy.
title: Leaving for Startup

text: The day Synth.io got acquired by Nexus Corp in 2022. Mixed feelings — we built something great but the startup culture was going to change. Turned out okay though, the team stayed mostly intact and we got more resources.
title: Synth.io Acquisition by Nexus

text: In distributed systems, the CAP theorem states that a distributed data store can provide at most two of three guarantees simultaneously: Consistency, Availability, and Partition tolerance. In practice, partition tolerance is non-negotiable, so the real choice is between CP and AP systems.
title: CAP Theorem

text: The perfect ratio for sourdough bread: 80% hydration, 20% starter relative to flour weight, 2% salt. Bulk ferment 4-6 hours at room temp with stretch and folds every 30 minutes for the first 2 hours. Cold retard overnight for flavor development.
title: Sourdough Bread Recipe

text: Isaac Asimov's Foundation series explores the concept of psychohistory — a mathematical science that predicts the future of large populations. The Seldon Plan spans a thousand years of galactic history. Read the trilogy in college and it changed how I think about emergent behavior in complex systems.
title: Asimov Foundation Series

text: Raft consensus algorithm: leader election uses randomized timeouts (150-300ms). Log replication requires majority acknowledgment. Safety guarantee: at most one leader per term. Much easier to understand than Paxos but equivalent in terms of safety properties.
title: Raft Consensus Algorithm

text: For Thai green curry paste from scratch: pound together green chilies, lemongrass, galangal, shallots, garlic, cilantro roots, white peppercorns, coriander seeds, cumin, shrimp paste, and kaffir lime zest. The mortar and pestle method gives better texture than a food processor.
title: Thai Green Curry Paste Recipe

text: The Three-Body Problem by Liu Cixin introduced the Dark Forest theory of the universe — civilizations stay silent because revealing your location to a technologically superior civilization is existential suicide. Terrifying but logically elegant.
title: Three-Body Problem Dark Forest

text: Bloom filters provide probabilistic set membership testing with zero false negatives but possible false positives. The optimal number of hash functions k = (m/n) * ln(2) where m is bit array size and n is number of elements. False positive rate approaches (1 - e^(-kn/m))^k.
title: Bloom Filter Theory

text: Got promoted to Senior Engineer today. Marcus told me in our 1:1 and I was genuinely speechless. Three years of grinding, the late nights on the reliability project, all the on-call rotations. Sam opened a bottle of champagne when I got home.
title: Promotion to Senior Engineer

text: Had a bad argument with Sam about how much time I spend working late. They said I was prioritizing code over our relationship. I got defensive and said something I regret about their career choices. We didn't talk for the rest of the evening.
title: Argument with Sam About Work

text: Surprise birthday party organized by Sam and the team at work. Walked into the conference room expecting a sprint planning meeting and everyone was there with cake and decorations. Even Leo and Mika drove in. I ugly cried a little.
title: Surprise Birthday Party

text: The morning we found out Byte was sick last year. She wasn't eating and was hiding under the bed. Rushed her to the emergency vet. Turned out to be a UTI, treatable with antibiotics, but those few hours of not knowing were awful.
title: Byte's Health Scare

text: Standing ovation at the company all-hands after presenting our team's reliability improvements — 99.99% uptime for six consecutive months. First time a team got recognized in front of the whole org. Felt proud of what we built together.
title: All-Hands Standing Ovation

text: Morning routine: wake up at 6:30, feed Byte, make pour-over coffee with the Hario V60 while listening to a tech podcast. Check Slack for any overnight alerts. Usually have about 20 minutes of quiet before Sam wakes up.
title: Morning Coffee Routine

text: Bike commute to the office takes about 25 minutes through Fremont. I like cutting through the Burke-Gilman trail section — less traffic and nicer scenery. Lock up at the garage, grab a coffee from the lobby cafe.
title: Bike Commute to Office

text: Lunch break usually at 12:30. Either heat up leftovers in the office kitchen or walk to the teriyaki place on 45th with whoever's around. Priya and I have been going together most days this week.
title: Lunch Break Routine

text: Evening wind-down: usually around 9pm I close the laptop, play with Byte using her feather toy for 10 minutes, then read sci-fi in bed for half an hour. Sam and I sometimes watch an episode of whatever show we're into before sleep.
title: Evening Wind-Down
